serverNeeds.description: Per-category audit scores: compute category-level scores server-side, serialize onto the audit result/snapshot, and make them suppression-aware on all effective-score exit points.
serverNeeds.evidence: rg 'categoryScore|category_score|perCategory|scoreByCategory' across server/ src/ shared/ returned zero hits; server/seo-audit.ts computes only siteScore + per-page score.
serverNeeds.whereItLives: server/seo-audit.ts (scoring) + server/audit-snapshot-views.ts (suppression-aware views); shared/types via src/components/audit/types.ts shapes.

serverNeeds.description: Category taxonomy remap: map the 5-value CheckCategory (content/technical/social/performance/accessibility) to the mockup's 6-cat model (index/onpage/perf/schema/links/mobile) via a server-side mapping, keeping check/category keys stable for suppressions, CSV export, and the client read path. Blocked on Q2.
serverNeeds.evidence: Per-check category map exists at server/audit-page.ts:36 (CHECK_CATEGORY) with the 5-value union at server/audit-page.ts:11 and src/components/audit/types.ts:8; no 6-cat model anywhere.
serverNeeds.whereItLives: server/audit-page.ts (mapping) + src/components/audit/types.ts; additive displayCategory field preferred over renaming persisted keys.

serverNeeds.description: Cross-workspace site-health aggregation (issue-type totals × client matrix from each workspace's latest effective snapshot) and, later, a cross-client batch-fix background job for sitehealth.js. Read-only aggregation endpoint first per Q10 default.
serverNeeds.evidence: All report reads are per-siteId (server/routes/reports.ts:252,264); rg -l 'cross.?workspace' server/ matches only unrelated modules (jobs, learnings, keyword cache) — no audit aggregation.
serverNeeds.whereItLives: New module, e.g. server/audit-rollup.ts (or server/domains/site-health/) reading listEffectiveSnapshotSummaries across workspaces; batch-fix via server/jobs.ts.

openQuestions.question: Q1: Which three screens does 'Site Audit 3→1' merge — Reading A (audit + Performance-triage + Links-triage as categories) or Reading B (SeoAudit's own 3 sub-tools redistributed)?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Reading A per audit.js:1-7's own comment; Reading B's redistribution happens anyway via the Parity Ledger moves.

openQuestions.question: Q2: Category taxonomy — server-side 5→6 mapping or UI-only regrouping? Client HealthTab, CSV export, and suppression keys all key on check/category.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Server-side additive displayCategory mapping (sn-site-audit-2), persisted keys unchanged.

openQuestions.question: Q3: Where does the real scheduled-audit config form live (mockup stubs Schedule as a toast)?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: F3 Drawer opened from the Site Audit toolbar Schedule button; reuses /api/audit-schedules as-is.

openQuestions.question: Q4: Snapshot Action Items + batch add-to-tasks — keep in Site Audit, fold into Inbox/Requests, or retire?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep both in Site Audit (action items inside History, batch-add in toolbar); endpoints already exist.

openQuestions.question: Q5: Confirm severity filter + search box are added additively to the rebuilt toolbar (mockup omits both).
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Add both (FilterChip + SearchField); pure client-side, zero server change.

openQuestions.question: Q6: Where does secondaryKeywords editing live (mockup Research shows one target keyword)?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Editor Research keyword panel keeps primary + secondary editing against the existing PATCH strategy pageMap.

openQuestions.question: Q7: Does bulk page analysis (cancellable background job) get a home or is it cut?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep as an Editor-toolbar background job; existing job semantics carry.

openQuestions.question: Q8: Does Keywords' local rank lenses + Local Presence fully replace the page-scoped local visibility read?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Accept keyword-scoped replacement; add a page-scoped panel to Editor Research only if the persona pass flags it.

openQuestions.question: Q9: Guide pattern — per-surface guides, one help center, or cut? (SeoAuditGuide + PageIntelligenceGuide homeless.)
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep per-surface Guide sub-tab (Schema precedent in the Parity Ledger).

openQuestions.question: Q10: sitehealth.js book roll-up + cross-client batch fix — scope and sequencing sign-off.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Read-only matrix first (sn-site-audit-3 aggregation endpoint); batch-fix job is a separate later phase with write-safety review.

openQuestions.question: Q11: Confirm the SEO Editor surface ticket adopts rows P1–P18 as acceptance criteria so PI doesn't fall between tickets.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Yes — SEO Editor ticket owns P1–P18; this ticket owns only the audit-side deep-link sender.

openQuestions.question: Q12: ?sub=content-decay senders must be retargeted in the same change that moves Content Health to Pipeline.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Treat as a hard acceptance criterion on the Pipeline move PR (two-halves contract), not a separate task.

kitNewFeatures.proposal: Audit graduation rule → Insights Engine ('plumbing stays plumbing until it earns a story')

kitNewFeatures.proposal: sitehealth.js book-level roll-up + cross-client batch fix
kitNewFeatures.rationale: Entirely new backend (sn-site-audit-3) + cross-client write-safety review; owner Q10 gates scope, read-only matrix could land first.

sitehealth.js internals were taken from the surface doc's description only (mockup file not re-read); the read-only-matrix quick-win perf claim (N-per-workspace fetch slow at book scale) is unverified.

verdicts.evidence: server/seo-audit.ts:23-34 (SeoAuditResult has only siteScore, no category-level score); server/seo-audit.ts:224,263 (site-level siteScore computed); page-level score at server/audit-page.ts:467 (computePageScore); rg for categoryScore|category_score|perCategory|scoreByCategory across server/ src/ shared/ returned zero hits. Suppression-aware exit points operate only on siteScore: server/audit-snapshot-views.ts:21,57-60 and server/audit-suppression-projection.ts:17 (getEffectiveAudit) — none carry per-category scores.
verdicts.correction: Claim stands. Per-category audit scores do not exist. Note the effective-score exit points to make suppression-aware are concrete: getEffectiveAudit (audit-suppression-projection.ts:17), getEffectivePreviousScore/toEffectiveAuditSnapshot (audit-snapshot-views.ts:14,33,57), applySuppressionsToAudit (seo-audit-suppressions.ts:16), AND the new cross-workspace read path GET /api/workspace-overview (workspaces.ts:118-129) which also serializes siteScore — any category-score field would need serializing there too (see sn-site-audit-3).

verdicts.evidence: server/audit-page.ts:11 (5-value CheckCategory union: content|technical|social|performance|accessibility); server/audit-page.ts:36 (CHECK_CATEGORY per-check map) and :463 auto-assign at audit-run time; src/components/audit/types.ts:8 mirrors the 5-value union and :86 CATEGORY_CONFIG. rg for the 6-cat mockup labels (index|onpage|schema|links|mobile as categories) found no 6-category model anywhere. Category is COMPUTED at audit-run and stored in snapshot JSON per-issue (audit-page.ts:463), so an additive displayCategory remap can be applied on read with no migration.
verdicts.correction: Claim stands as written including effort S. The 'partial' assessment (per-check map exists, no 6-cat model) is accurate, and the additive-displayCategory-over-renaming-persisted-keys approach is correct because the existing category is persisted in snapshot blobs — renaming would break historical reads. Effort S holds ONLY if implemented as a pure read-side/audit-time mapping with no snapshot-schema change; the plan's stated preference keeps it there.

verdicts.evidence: A cross-workspace audit-score aggregation endpoint ALREADY EXISTS: GET /api/workspace-overview (server/routes/workspaces.ts:107-138) iterates listVisibleWorkspaces() and, per workspace, reads getLatestEffectiveSnapshot(ws.webflowSiteId, ws.auditSuppressions) and serializes { score:siteScore, totalPages, errors, warnings, previousScore, lastAuditDate } — read-only, suppression-aware, multi-workspace. All PER-SITE report reads confirmed at server/routes/reports.ts:252,264. site-health-slice (server/intelligence/site-health-slice.ts:100) is strictly single-workspace (every fn takes one workspaceId), so it does NOT provide this. No issue-type-total or client-matrix aggregation exists (rg clientMatrix|issueTypeTotals|cross-workspace audit = zero relevant hits).
verdicts.correction: Direction (aggregation needs building) is right but the framing 'existsToday: no / New module' is wrong on two counts. (1) A cross-workspace, suppression-aware, read-only audit-score roll-up ALREADY ships at GET /api/workspace-overview (workspaces.ts:107) — the natural home is to EXTEND this existing endpoint's per-workspace audit block, not a brand-new server/audit-rollup.ts. (2) What genuinely does NOT exist is the specific SHAPE claimed: issue-type totals (per-check-type breakdown) and a client×issue-type matrix — workspace-overview only emits scalar siteScore + errors/warnings counts per workspace, no per-check-type dimension. So: aggregation SCAFFOLD exists (reuse it); the issue-type×client MATRIX and the batch-fix background job do not. Effort L is still plausible for the matrix + batch-fix job, but the read-only aggregation half is closer to M given the existing endpoint to extend. Threading a category-score serialization here also ties this to sn-site-audit-1's exit-point list.
